name: Cheddar-Pepper Biscuits
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium bowl, combine the flour, baking powder, salt, and pepper.
2. Cut in the butter with a pastry blender or two knives until the mixture is the consistency of coarse crumbs.
3. Stir in 1 cup of Cheddar cheese.
4. Add milk; mix just until the dry ingredients are moistened.
5. Turn the dough out onto a floured surface and knead gently, about 8 to 10 turns.
6. Roll out or pat the dough to be about 1/2-inch thick.
7. Cut with a 2-1/2-inch biscuit cutter. Re-roll and cut scraps.
8. Place on a greased baking sheet.
9. Sprinkle with the remaining 1/2 cup of Cheddar cheese.
10. Bake at 450°F for 10 to 12 minutes, or until golden brown. Yield: 12 to 14 biscuits.
11. Calories: 168; Fat: 10g
